_comment: GENERIC, non-sensitive EXAMPLE procedure only (repo stays template-level; no personal/sensitive protocols in git). A textbook recrystallization of benzoic acid — a common, safe teaching prep — used to demo the step+hazard+amount format. Real procedures are supplied by the chemist and pushed from their PC, never committed. Format: 'scale' gives a target for stoichiometry hints; each step may name a 'reagent' (resolved to PubChem GHS + MW) and a target 'mol' or 'mass_g'.
name: Recrystallization of benzoic acid (example)

steps.step: Weigh crude benzoic acid into a clean Erlenmeyer flask.
Add the minimum boiling water to dissolve; heat on a hot plate.
Add decolorizing charcoal if the solution is coloured, then hot-filter.
Cool the filtrate slowly to room temperature, then in an ice bath, to crystallize.
Vacuum-filter the crystals, wash with a little cold water, and dry.
